headline: Sabah Wildlife Corridor Loses 28,000 Hectares to Clearing
context: WWF-Malaysia satellite analysis revealed 28,000 hectares of Kinabatangan Wildlife Corridor deforested between 2020 and 2025 despite protected status.

cards.big: 28,000 hectares of protected Kinabatangan corridor deforested in 5 years
cards.sub: Wildlife connectivity between Sabah key habitats critically compromised

cards.big: Borneo pygmy elephant population in corridor fell from 1,500 to 1,100
cards.sub: Habitat fragmentation forced elephants into plantations causing 45 conflicts

cards.big: Sabah Forestry issued only 3 enforcement actions against 18 violations
cards.sub: Logging companies cited provisional licenses predating corridor protected status

cards.big: International donors pledged RM220 million for corridor restoration in 2025
cards.sub: Replanting efforts cover only 4,200 hectares per year at current funding levels

cards.big: Corridor loss tests whether protected status means protection or just a label
28,000 hectares of wildlife corridor lost. Sabah's orangutan habitat cannot survive another decade of clearing.
